page: Vultr voc-s-24c-192gb-3840s-amd is an instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family. It features 24 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is configured with 24 physical cores and a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio under a shared CPU allocation model. It includes 192.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and is bundled with 3840 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.

In benchmark evaluations, this server demonstrates strong performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance scoring 2839.58 and multi-core CPU performance reaching 67368.14, both placing in the top 25% of tested systems. Because it utilizes a shared CPU allocation, the instance offers a highly cost-efficient profile for workloads requiring substantial memory and storage capacity without the premium cost of dedicated CPU cores. The high resource density of 192.0 GB RAM and 3840 GB NVMe SSD provides a favorable balance of memory and storage capacity per resource unit.

This hardware configuration is well-suited for memory-intensive applications, large-scale database hosting, data caching layers, and enterprise web serving. The combination of high capacity NVMe storage and 192.0 GB of RAM supports heavy read/write operations and large datasets, making it appropriate for relational databases, NoSQL deployments, and analytical processing workloads that do not require constant, dedicated 100% CPU utilization.
description: Vultr voc-s-24c-192gb-3840s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 24 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC-Rome processor with an x86_64 architecture. Operating at 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation, this server is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM and 3840 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmark results indicate strong processing capabilities, with stress-ng single-core performance at 2839.58 and multi-core performance at 67368.14, both ranking in the top 25%. The instance provides a cost-efficient option for resource-heavy deployments by balancing massive memory and storage capacities against a shared CPU model. It is highly suitable for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, and large-scale data processing workloads.

tagline: A high-capacity memory and NVMe storage instance featuring twenty-four AMD EPYC cores for data-intensive and database workloads.
